creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Roman Bogorodskiy from comment #1)

> pkg info|grep -i weben

Today, after installing (as automatic) py39-qt6-webengine-6.5.0 and py39-wheel-0.41.2: 


% pkg info|grep -i weben
falkon-23.08.0                 Web browser based on Webengine and Qt Framework
py39-qt5-webengine-5.15.6_2    Python bindings for the Qt5 toolkit, QtWebEngine module
py39-qt6-webengine-6.5.0       Python bindings for the Qt6 toolkit, QtWebEngine module
py39-webencodings-0.5.1        Character encoding aliases for legacy web content
qt5-webengine-5.15.8_5         Qt 5 library to render web content
qt6-webengine-6.5.2_3          Qt 6 library to render web content
% 


A dialogue at application launch time: 

----
Chromium/QtWebEngine upgrade detected:

You are upgrading to QtWebEngine 6.5.2 but used Qt 5 for the last qutebrowser launch.

Data managed by Chromium will be upgraded. This is a one-way operation: If you open qutebrowser with Qt 5 again later, any Chromium data will be invalid and discarded.

This affects page data such as cookies, but not data managed by qutebrowser, such as your configuration or :open history.
The affected data is in /home/grahamperrin/.local/share/qutebrowser/webengine.

Qt wrapper info:
PyQt6: success
PyQt5: not imported
-> selected: PyQt6 (via autoselect)

You can use --qt-wrapper or set QUTE_QT_WRAPPER in your environment to override this.
----

After clicking OK, qutebrowser-2.5.4_1 presents the change log for v3.0.0. This might confuse someone who's not aware that the application has not been upgraded (according to what's changed), but I'm fine with it. 

py39-qt6-webengine-6.5.0 and py39-wheel-0.41.2 built with poudriere.
